AppEntry.description:
A single Prowlarr application connection (D-03-03).

Encodes the declarative YAML model: one entry per Sonarr/Radarr instance
that Prowlarr should sync to. The reconciler (Plan 05) resolves
``api_key_env`` via ``os.environ`` at runtime — the API key value itself
NEVER appears in this YAML.

api_key_env.description: Name of the environment variable holding the target's API key.
api_key_env.title: Api Key Env

base_url.description: HTTP URL Prowlarr uses to reach the target (e.g. http://sonarr.svc:8989).
base_url.title: Base Url

name.description: Display name of the connection in Prowlarr (match key, D-03-03).
name.title: Name

sync_level.description: Prowlarr syncLevel (mirror of the field in /api/v1/applications).
sync_level.title: Sync Level

AppEntry.title: AppEntry

AppsSection.description: Prowlarr app-sync section (D-03-02 — only resource type Prowlarr reconciles).
AppsSection.title: AppsSection

CategoriesSection.description:
List of qBittorrent categories with opt-in prune.

NEVER set prune=True in production — cleanuparr depends on the
'cleanuparr-unlinked' category surviving reconciliation (R-04 in RESEARCH.md).

prune.description: Opt-in deletion of unmanaged categories (D-04). NEVER set true — cleanuparr depends on cleanuparr-unlinked (R-04).
prune.title: Prune

CategoriesSection.title: CategoriesSection

Category.description:
A qBittorrent category (POST /api/v2/torrents/createCategory).

Pitfall 3 (RESEARCH.md line 851): qBit treats empty savePath as
"use default save path" (/data/complete in cluster). The reconciler
MUST send the explicit savePath in createCategory/editCategory.

extra="allow" — qBit 5.1+ may add a download_path field on GET; we
accept it on the way in but never write it out (only name + savePath
go into form-encoded POST bodies).

name.description: Category name (stable match key for differ).
name.title: Name

savePath.description: qBit-side path where torrents in this category land. MUST be explicit (e.g. /data/anime), never empty.
savePath.title: Savepath

Category.title: Category

ContentRoutingRule.description:
A single content_tags routing rule (Phase 6, D-06-RETAG-01).

Genre-keyword-driven post-import retagger rule. `tag` MUST exist in
`instance.tags.items` (reconciler enforces via ReconcileError).
`keywords` is case-insensitive substring-matched against `item.genres[]`
on each series/movie. Phase 6 default config (Plan 06-06) ships:
  - Sonarr family: ["Family", "Kids", "Children"]   (NOT Animation — too broad)
  - Sonarr anime:  ["Anime"]                         (TVDB first-class genre)
  - Radarr family: ["Family"]                        (NO Animation — catches Pixar/Disney)
  - Radarr anime:  (no rule)                          (TMDB has no Anime genre)

keywords.description: Genre keywords (case-insensitive substring match against item.genres[]).
keywords.title: Keywords

tag.description: Tag label to apply (must exist in instance.tags.items).
tag.title: Tag

ContentRoutingRule.title: ContentRoutingRule

ContentRoutingSection.description:
content_tags step config (Phase 6, D-06-RETAG-01, step 10).

Opt-in (enable=False default) — reconciler logs `content_tags_reconcile_skipped`
and returns when disabled. Runs AFTER series_tags/movie_tags (D-05-ORDER-01 +
Phase 6 ordering extension — step 10).
ContentRoutingSection.title: ContentRoutingSection

DownloadClient.description:
A Sonarr download client (qBittorrent, Transmission, ...).

Matched by ``name`` (D-20). Read-only fields excluded from diff (D-21).
Uses ``extra="allow"`` for forward-compat with future Sonarr versions
(Phase 1 simplification — Pitfall 4 trade-off documented in RESEARCH.md).

configContract.description: Sonarr config contract (e.g., 'QBittorrentSettings'). Must match implementation.
configContract.title: Configcontract

name.description: Display name (matching key, must be unique).
name.title: Name

priority.description: Lower = preferred when multiple clients match.
priority.title: Priority

tag_labels.description: Human-readable tag labels (e.g. ['tv', 'anime']). The reconciler resolves these to integer IDs in instance.tags after the tags-reconcile step (D-05-ORDER-01). Excluded from API diff/dump — never sent to Sonarr.
tag_labels.title: Tag Labels

tags.description: Tag IDs — set by the reconciler after label→id resolution.
tags.title: Tags

DownloadClient.title: DownloadClient

DownloadClientsSection.description: A list of download_clients with opt-in prune (D-04).
DownloadClientsSection.title: DownloadClientsSection

FieldKV.description:
Generic key-value used in Sonarr's ``fields[]`` array (qBit-specific settings).

Source: Sonarr OpenAPI #/components/schemas/Field. Sonarr ad-hoc adds
UI-metadata keys (``label``, ``helpText``, ``selectOptions``...) so we
accept ``extra="allow"`` and exclude these from diffs (Pitfall 2).

value.description: Field value — type depends on field name.
value.title: Value

FieldKV.title: FieldKV

HostConfigSection.description:
Opt-in host_config reconciliation (D-03-04).

``enable`` defaults to ``False`` — the reconciler logs ``host_config_reconcile_skipped``
and returns without calling GET /config/host. This prevents an accidental
YAML typo from locking arrconf out of the app via authenticationRequired
misconfiguration. To activate host_config reconciliation, set
``host_config: { enable: true }`` explicitly in the YAML config.

Only the safe-to-reconcile subset of host_config fields is modeled — the
full HostConfig model (Plan 01) has more fields but they are either
server-derived or credential-sensitive (apiKey / password) and excluded
with ``exclude=True`` on the resource model.

enable.description: Opt-in flag — host_config reconcile is skipped unless this is True (D-03-04).
enable.title: Enable

HostConfigSection.title: HostConfigSection

Indexer.description:
A Sonarr/Radarr indexer entry (created by Prowlarr sync, not by arrconf directly).

Phase 3 reconciles indexers for sync alignment only (D-03-02: arrconf does
not manage indexer definitions — Prowlarr does). The standard diff/PUT
pattern still applies: cluster state is compared against YAML desired
state, with WR-01 credential safety on the apiKey field.

name.description: Display name (matching key, must be unique).
name.title: Name

Indexer.title: Indexer

IndexersSection.description: A list of indexers with opt-in prune (D-04).
IndexersSection.title: IndexersSection

MovieTagsSection.description:
D-05-SPLIT-02: retroactive default-tag for untagged movies in Radarr.

Mirror of SeriesTagsSection with default_tag='movies' (Radarr convention:
'movies' not 'tv', matching qBit category 'radarr-movies').

default_tag.description: Tag label added to un-tagged movies (D-05-SPLIT-02).
default_tag.title: Default Tag

enable.description: Default-ON: tag un-tagged movies with default_tag (D-05-SPLIT-02).
enable.title: Enable

MovieTagsSection.title: MovieTagsSection

Notification.description:
A Sonarr/Radarr notification entry (Discord, Slack, webhook, custom script, ...).

Matched by ``name`` (D-20). ``on*`` event flags differ between Sonarr and
Radarr — captured via extra="allow" so the same Notification class works
for both apps without app-specific subclassing.
Notification.title: Notification

NotificationsSection.description: A list of notifications with opt-in prune (D-04).
NotificationsSection.title: NotificationsSection

PreferencesSection.description:
qBittorrent preferences opt-in reconcile (D-03-04 mirror for qBit).

``enable`` defaults to False — same opt-in pattern as host_config (D-03-04).
When disabled, the reconciler logs 'qbit_preferences_reconcile_skipped'.
Only the 4-key allowlist (QbitPreferences) is written; all other qBit
settings remain operator-controlled.

enable.description: Opt-in flag — qBit preferences reconcile is skipped unless True (D-03-04 mirror).
enable.title: Enable

PreferencesSection.title: PreferencesSection

ProwlarrInstance.description:
A single Prowlarr instance (Phase 3, D-03-02 — app sync only).

Indexer definitions (the catalog of newsnab / torznab endpoints) remain
managed in the Prowlarr UI — they are out of Phase 3 scope.
ProwlarrInstance.title: ProwlarrInstance

QbitPreferences.description:
4-key allowlist for qBit setPreferences (D-05-QBT-02 + Q2).

qBit's /api/v2/app/preferences returns ~80 keys. Most are operator-
controlled (UI theme, port, bandwidth limits). arrconf manages
ONLY the 4 keys below — adding more requires an explicit decision.

extra="forbid" enforces this at the YAML layer: an operator who
drops a stray `max_active_downloads:` in YAML gets a clear
ValidationError pointing at the unknown key.

Pitfall 4 (RESEARCH.md line 858): the reconciler MUST serialize
booleans as JSON-typed `true`/`false`, NOT as quoted strings.
QbitPreferences.title: QbitPreferences

QbittorrentInstance.description:
A single qBittorrent instance (Phase 5, D-05-QBT-02).

Cookie-based auth (D-05-QBT-01): credentials come from QBT_USER / QBT_PASS
env vars (CLAUDE.md §"Variables d'environnement"), NOT from this YAML.
Resources managed: categories (6 entries) + preferences (opt-in settings).
QbittorrentInstance.title: QbittorrentInstance

RadarrInstance.description:
A single Radarr instance (Phase 3, D-03-01 — full parity with SonarrInstance).

Identical section list to SonarrInstance — Radarr's v3 API exposes the
same resource types with the same shapes. The reconciler (Plan 04) is a
parallel implementation; the YAML schema is intentionally aligned.

Phase 5 additions (D-05-SPLIT-02, D-05-PATHMAP-01, D-05-MIG-01):
- tags: declarative Radarr tags (movies, anime, family)
- remote_path_mappings: qBit-to-Radarr path translation entries
- movie_tags: retroactive default-tag for un-tagged movies (default_tag='movies')

Phase 6 (D-06-RETAG-01): content_routing — genre-keyword retagger (step 10)
RadarrInstance.title: RadarrInstance

RemotePathMapping.description:
A *arr Remote Path Mapping (GET /api/v3/remotepathmapping).

Pitfall 6 (RESEARCH.md line 872): both remotePath AND localPath
MUST end with '/'. Sonarr does literal prefix-replacement; without
the trailing slash the import-time path translation silently fails.
The reconciler's smoke test enforces this invariant.
RemotePathMapping.title: RemotePathMapping

RemotePathMappingsSection.description:
List of *arr Remote Path Mappings with opt-in prune (D-05-PATHMAP-01).

Match key is the composite tuple (host, remotePath) — changes are DELETE+ADD
(no PUT endpoint on the *arr API, Pitfall 1).

prune.description: Opt-in deletion of unmanaged path mappings (D-04).
prune.title: Prune

RemotePathMappingsSection.title: RemotePathMappingsSection

RootFolder.description: A Sonarr/Radarr root folder. ``path`` is the stable identity (D-03-01).
RootFolder.title: RootFolder

RootFoldersSection.description:
A list of root folders with opt-in prune (D-04).

Sonarr/Radarr have NO PUT endpoint for root folders — path changes produce
DELETE + ADD via the differ (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 1).
RootFoldersSection.title: RootFoldersSection

SeerrInstance.description:
A single Seerr instance (Phase 6, D-06-SCOPE-01 — single-instance per ADR-7).

Resources reconciled (per D-06-SCOPE-01 minimum-viable):
- sonarr_service: PUT /api/v1/settings/sonarr/{id} (match by isDefault=true)
- radarr_service: PUT /api/v1/settings/radarr/{id} (match by isDefault=true)
- users.admin: PUT /api/v1/user/{id} (single admin, id=1 per current cluster)
- main_settings: POST /api/v1/settings/main (scoped subset)
SeerrInstance.title: SeerrInstance

enable.description: Opt-in default-ON for the scoped subset.
enable.title: Enable

SeerrRadarrServiceSection.description:
Seerr -> Radarr service connection config (D-06-SCOPE-01).

NO animeTags/activeAnime* — research-verified absence on Seerr-side Radarr config.
Family/anime routing for movies is handled entirely by Plan 06-05's content_tags step.
SeerrRadarrServiceSection.title: SeerrRadarrServiceSection

SeerrSonarrServiceSection.description:
Seerr -> Sonarr service connection config (D-06-SCOPE-01).

apiKey is intentionally absent from this YAML schema — operator bootstraps
Seerr->Sonarr/Radarr connections via Seerr UI ONCE (D-06-CREDS-01) and the
reconciler preserves the cluster apiKey on every PUT.

activeAnimeDirectory.description: Sonarr root folder path for anime requests.
activeAnimeDirectory.title: Activeanimedirectory

activeAnimeProfileId.description: Sonarr quality profile integer ID for anime (Phase 5 'Anime' profile).
activeAnimeProfileId.title: Activeanimeprofileid

activeDirectory.description: Sonarr root folder path for non-anime requests.
activeDirectory.title: Activedirectory

animeTags.description: Sonarr tag integer IDs applied to anime requests routed via Seerr.
animeTags.title: Animetags

tags.description: Sonarr tag integer IDs applied to non-anime requests routed via Seerr.
tags.title: Tags

SeerrSonarrServiceSection.title: SeerrSonarrServiceSection

prune.description: Opt-in deletion (D-04). Never True in Phase 6.
prune.title: Prune

SeriesTagsSection.description:
D-05-MIG-01: retroactive default-tag for untagged series in Sonarr.

``enable`` defaults to True — Phase 5 core feature. On each apply run,
arrconf adds ``default_tag`` to any series that has no tags. Idempotent:
series already tagged are not modified.

default_tag.description: Tag label added to un-tagged series (D-05-MIG-01).
default_tag.title: Default Tag

enable.description: Default-ON: tag un-tagged series with default_tag (D-05-MIG-01).
enable.title: Enable

SeriesTagsSection.title: SeriesTagsSection

SonarrInstance.description:
A single Sonarr instance (Phase 3 extends Phase 1's download_clients-only scope).

Resource section order is alphabetical for readability — runtime
reconcile ordering is enforced by the reconciler itself (Plan 03).

Phase 5 additions (D-05-SPLIT-01, D-05-PATHMAP-01, D-05-MIG-01):
- tags: declarative Sonarr tags (tv, anime, family)
- remote_path_mappings: qBit-to-Sonarr path translation entries
- series_tags: retroactive default-tag for un-tagged series

Phase 6 (D-06-RETAG-01): content_routing — genre-keyword retagger (step 10)
SonarrInstance.title: SonarrInstance

TagItem.description:
A single Sonarr/Radarr tag declared in YAML (Phase 5, D-05-SPLIT-01).

Minimal model: only ``label`` is needed for match + create. The server-
derived ``id`` lives on the ``Tag`` resource model in resources/sonarr/tag.py.
TagItem.title: TagItem

TagsSection.description: List of Sonarr/Radarr tags with opt-in prune (D-05-SPLIT-01).
TagsSection.title: TagsSection

description:
Top-level arrconf YAML schema (root for JSON Schema generation).

Phase 3 (D-03-05): flat ``sonarr`` / ``radarr`` / ``prowlarr`` dicts at the
root level. The Phase-1 ``apps:`` indirection has been removed — every
caller updated atomically with this rewrite (Plan 02 Task 2.3).

Phase 5 (D-05-QBT-02): adds ``qbittorrent`` dict following the same flat-root
convention (D-03-05). RootConfig still uses ``extra='forbid'`` to reject typos.

Phase 6 (D-06-SCOPE-01): adds ``seerr`` dict following the same flat-root convention.
title: RootConfig
